content.full_text:
Employee time off - contribution

Palmer Madden called with the following question:

If any employee is allowed (by an employer) to take time off to work on a campaign with the understanding that the employee will, at some future time, make up the time, is that employer making an in-kind contribution? Or, is it an in-kind loan? Or is it nothing?

He did cite a section of the Federal Elections Code which, evidently makes allowances for this type time off without any reporting obligation.

Answer: In-kind contribution unless this "make-up" procedure is a uniform policy for all employees.
